review: MANUAL-VERIFICATION Stage 2 — Cluster D (SubscriptionGate fail-closed + billing reachability)

vitest_result.fail_before_reproduced: Swapped subscription-gate.tsx + dashboard/layout.tsx back to parent 3b8a08d content (new test files kept) and re-ran: exactly the 4 new tests fail (3 in subscription-gate.test.tsx, 1 in settings page.test.tsx), 8 pre-existing tests still pass — matches fixer's fail-before claim, confirms non-tautological. Reverted cleanly (git checkout --, git status clean, git diff HEAD empty) before continuing. [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE 2026-07-18T03:54:57Z]

reasons: apps/web/src/components/subscription-gate.tsx:32-55 (isGateExempt) and :63-118 (SubscriptionGate): fail-closed logic is sound. useEffect early-returns without setState when `exempt` is true (:70-71 in diff, guard `if (exempt) return;`); otherwise on catch() it now sets state='error' (was 'allowed' pre-fix) — never 'allowed' on a fetch failure. Render order is: `if (exempt) return children` -> `if (loading) spinner` -> `if (gated) Paywall` -> `if (error) EntitlementError` -> fallback `return children`. The fallback is reachable ONLY when state==='allowed', i.e. entitlement resolved successfully and the user is not gated. There is no code path where an errored/aborted/timed-out entitlement request reaches `return <>{children}</>` for a non-exempt route. Confirmed by reverting to pre-fix source with the new tests still in place: the 'FAILS CLOSED' test fails against old code (renders 'allowed'/children) and passes against the fix.

EntitlementError (subscription-gate.tsx, new function, data-testid='subscription-gate-error') is a distinct state from Paywall: it does not claim the user is unsubscribed ('We could not verify your subscription... this is usually temporary'), offers a 'Try again' button wired to reloadKey which re-triggers the effect (re-adds 'exempt'/'reloadKey' to the dependency array), and links to /pricing and /dashboard/settings. This correctly avoids falsely paywalling a paying user hit by a transient error while never revealing gated content — the design the brief asked me to sanity-check is sound.

GATE_EXEMPT_PREFIXES (subscription-gate.tsx:47) = ['/dashboard/settings'] only. isGateExempt(null) returns false at line 51 (`if (!pathname) return false;`) — safe default confirmed by direct source read, not just by the test asserting it. grep across apps/web/src shows isGateExempt/GATE_EXEMPT_PREFIXES referenced nowhere else — no second gate/allowlist to audit.

Enumerated all /dashboard/* route directories (agents, analytics, applications, approvals, cover-letters, email, interviews, jobs, networking, offers, resume, settings, stories, [...slug]) — only 'settings' matches the exempt prefix; none of the metered agent-run routes (jobs, resume, cover-letters, agents, etc.) are exempted, and the prefix match requires an exact match or a '/' boundary (`pathname.startsWith(`${prefix}/`)`), so a route like '/dashboard/settingsx' would NOT falsely match.

MV-mobile-dashboard-002: subscription-gate.tsx Paywall() now renders `<Link href="/dashboard/settings">manage your account</Link>` in place of the old dead-end plain text 'and manage your account.' — confirmed both in source and via a passing test asserting getByRole('link', {name:/manage your account/i}).getAttribute('href') === '/dashboard/settings'.

Tests are meaningful, not tautological: reverted source to parent commit while keeping the new test files and re-ran — exactly the 4 new assertions fail (fail-closed test, settings-reachable-when-gated test, mobile-link test, settings-page free-account test), the 8 pre-existing tests in the same files still pass. This rules out both a test that always passes and a test that accidentally exercises the same code path as an existing passing test.

No prohibited patterns in the diff: no `any`/`as any`, no @ts-ignore, no Math.random, no TODO/FIXME stubs. One `// eslint-disable-next-line import/first` was added in settings/__tests__/page.test.tsx for a new import placed after vi.mock() calls (required ordering for Vitest's hoisted mocks) — this duplicates an already-existing identical disable comment in the same file for the pre-existing `import SettingsPage from "../page"` line, i.e. it is the established convention in this test file for import-order-vs-mock-hoisting, not a new error-suppression pattern. Not counted as a violation.
concerns: SCOPE (not a security defect, but flagged per the brief's request to adversarially probe this): the exemption is at the /dashboard/settings ROUTE level, and that single route/page (settings-client.tsx, driven by apps/web/src/app/dashboard/settings/sections.ts:16-24) bundles 8 tabs — Profile, Resume Management, Portfolio Sync, Notifications, Agent Configuration, Integrations, Privacy & Compliance, and Billing & Subscription — behind one client-side tab switch, not separate Next.js routes. MV-pricing-003/MV-settings-003 only require that a free user can view plan/quota and reach 'Manage subscription' (i.e. the Billing tab). As shipped, exempting the whole route also gives a free/unpaid user full access to: uploading a new resume version (settings-client.tsx:162 uploadResume -> POST /resumes/upload, settings-client.tsx:384 wired to resume-upload-btn) and career-data re-ingestion of GitHub/portfolio/LinkedIn (settings-client.tsx:143 refreshCareerData -> POST /workspaces/career-data/refresh) — capabilities that were previously blocked for a genuinely free user by the blanket paywall on this same route, and that remain properly gated when reached via the OTHER route that does the same category of work if one existed. I verified this is NOT an exposure of metered agent OUTPUT — Resume Management only stores/uploads a resume file (extract_bullets in apps/api/app/services/resume_tailor.py is deterministic text parsing, not an LLM call) and Career Data sync only ingests the user's own public GitHub/portfolio data; no tailored-resume content, cover-letter drafts, job matches, or other paid agent-generated output is rendered anywhere in Settings, and every actionable agent endpoint (apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:539 _require_active_subscription) remains hard-gated server-side regardless of this frontend exemption. So this is a scope/product-boundary question, not a security hole or a paywall bypass for the product's actual paid deliverable. Recommend the orchestrator either (a) get an explicit, documented product-owner ruling that free access to Resume Management/Career Data/Agent-Config-preferences/Notifications/Integrations/Privacy via Settings is acceptable (the same treatment already given to the D1/H-4 items in ADR-MV-02), or (b) a tighter follow-up that scopes the exemption to only the Billing tab content (e.g. a dedicated always-exempt sub-route, or a gate check inside SettingsClient that still paywalls non-billing tabs for a truly gated user). This did not block PASS because it is a business-scope question rather than a security/regression risk, and none of the checklist's core security asks (fail-closed, no gated-content leak) are violated by it — but it should not be treated as silently closed either.

Minor/no-op: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/layout.tsx diff (lines 12-14) is docstring-only, no behavior change — verified via `git diff 430f87f^ 430f87f -- apps/web/src/app/dashboard/layout.tsx` showing only comment lines added.

Not verified in this review (out of stated scope): production reachability / backend pytest — task brief explicitly scoped this review to frontend vitest/tsc/next build only. No backend pytest was run. This review makes no claim about backend behavior beyond reading (not executing) apps/api/app/routers/agents.py and resumes.py to confirm _require_active_subscription's placement, which is [INFERRED] from source reading, not [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] since no pytest was executed per the brief's instruction.
